theory_text: Reverberation of sound is when sound reflects and reflected sound overlap. This effect is highly undesirable in speeches and in halls and concerts so to finish reverberation curved sound board is placed behind the stage to make sound reach every where evenly without reverberation. Also an cielinge and in some places sound at absorbing materials are placed to reduce the reverberation by absorbing sound.

diagram_json.figure: Methods for controlling reverberation in a hall
relationships: Curved soundboard reflects sound waves to achieve even distribution
Sound absorbing material on the ceiling absorbs sound waves to reduce reverberation
connectors: Arrows illustrate the path of sound waves reflecting off a curved surface
A bracket connects the curved soundboard diagram to the concept of evenly distributed sound
Arrows depict sound waves hitting and being absorbed by ceiling material

page_summary: The page presents an answer to a question about reverberation of sound. It includes a detailed handwritten explanation and two diagrams illustrating methods to control reverberation: one showing a curved soundboard for even sound distribution, and another depicting sound-absorbing material on a ceiling to reduce echoes.
